title: Adversarial verification layer + contradiction detection + multi-source rule for high-stakes claims
context: 'Verified' must not be confused with 'true': ATLTUAE adversarially checks each claim, surfaces contradictions rather than hiding them, and requires multiple independent license-clean sources for high-stakes claims before they may appear even as informational. This safety-critical M1 task reuses the existing deep-research harness pattern (fan-out -> fetch -> adversarially verify -> synthesize) rather than inventing a new per-claim verifier.
objective: Implement the adversarial verification layer: each claim is steel-manned and checked against disconfirming sources (not accepted on a single agreeing source), contradictions across the claim graph are surfaced, and high-stakes claims require multiple independent license-clean sources before surfacing, reusing the deep-research fan-out/fetch/verify/synthesize pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Each claim is adversarially checked (steel-man + seek disconfirming sources), not accepted on a single agreeing source; contradictions across the claim graph are surfaced, not hidden
High-stakes claims require multiple independent, license-clean sources before they may be surfaced even as 'informational'
Reuses the deep-research harness pattern (fan-out -> fetch -> adversarially verify -> synthesize)
Reviewed and signed off by the gate reviewer (recorded)
